message.content: go

addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - API usage, tool use, Anthropic SDK usage. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Bash, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

- Explore: Fast read-only search agent for locating code. Use it to find files by pattern (eg. "src/components/**/*.tsx"), grep for symbols or keywords (eg. "API endpoints"), or answer "where is X defined / which files reference Y." Do NOT use it for code review, design-doc auditing, cross-file consistency checks, or open-ended analysis — it reads excerpts rather than whole files and will miss content past its read window. When calling, specify search breadth: "quick" for a single targeted lookup, "medium" for moderate exploration, or "very thorough" to search across multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

# Todo

This file contains near term tasks with a short description
and reference links to more details.

## In Progress

A bulleted list of the in-progress task's development "ladder":
   - 0.xx.y-0 blah (done)
   - 0.xx.y-1 blah blah (current)
   - 0.xx.y-2 blah blah blah
   - 0.xx.y close-out and validation

**Subcommand trait sweep** — collapse the per-arm
`Context::load` + `try_from` + `run_command` boilerplate in
`main.rs` behind a `Subcommand` trait. Design in
[chores-10.md](chores-10.md#chore-open-subcommand-trait-sweep-0500-0).
Per-step evaluation gate: any substep may modify the shape
significantly or abandon the cycle.

- 0.50.0-0 plan + version bump + chores section + todo ladder
  + linkme/inventory todos (done)
- 0.50.0-1 add `subcommand.rs` (trait) + port `chid` (done)
- 0.50.0-1.1 dicom-rs gotchas → todo (done)
- 0.50.0-1.2 --exec doc + matches! → match (done)
- 0.50.0-2..N port remaining 11 subcommands (groupings TBD)
- 0.50.0-K main.rs dispatch rework
- 0.50.0 close-out

## Todo

A markdown list of tasks to do in the near future, ordered
highest-priority first. Keep entries brief — 1-3 lines.
Detailed motivation, safety requirements, and ordering belong
in `notes/chores-NN.md` design subsections; link via `[N]` ref.

Items use lazy numbering — every entry begins with `1. `; the
markdown renderer auto-numbers them, so reorder/insert without
renumbering. Reference by displayed number ("let's work on #3").
1. **single-field `options_flags` leaves → `value` field.**
   `0.47.0` introduced the convention (single-field leaf names
   its field `value`, declares the flag via `#[arg(long = "…")]`,
   so consumers read `args.<leaf>.value` not `args.<leaf>.<leaf>`)
   on the new `squash` leaf. Sweep the pre-existing single-field
   leaves to match: `scope`, `repo`, `dry_run`, `private`,
   `account`, `config`, `use_template` + their consumers
   (`init.rs`, tests).
1. **Investigate `linkme` for subcommand registration.**
   Distributed-slice registry — each subcommand registers itself
   at link time; `main.rs` discovers them via the slice rather
   than matching a `Commands` enum. Reduces per-subcommand
   touchpoints from 3 (mod decl + enum variant + match arm) to 1
   (registration). Costs: loses compile-time exhaustiveness
   (missing registration = runtime gap); help-output ordering
   depends on link order unless sorted; macro-magic dependency.
   Revisit once the `0.50.0` trait sweep's per-arm cost has been
   felt under real "add a subcommand" load.
   <https://github.com/dtolnay/linkme>
1. **Investigate `inventory` as `linkme` alternative.** Same
   shape as `linkme` — runtime-iterable registry populated by

content.content:
679	## chore: open Subcommand trait sweep (0.50.0-0)
680	
681	Commits: [[13]]
682	
683	Multi-step. The 12 subcommand match arms in `main.rs` repeat
684	the same `Context::load` + `try_from(&args)` + `run_command`
685	boilerplate — ~12 lines per arm. This cycle introduces a
686	`Subcommand` trait so each arm collapses to a single
687	`dispatch::<T>(args, cli.log)` line while keeping the
688	`Commands` enum as the dispatch source of truth (compile-time
689	exhaustiveness preserved). Plan-only opener; the trait + first
690	worked-example port land in `-1`.
691	
692	### Trait shape (initial sketch)
693	
694	- `Subcommand` trait on each subcommand's `Args` type:
695	  - `type Params`
696	  - `fn into_params(&self) -> Result<Self::Params, String>`
697	    — total `From` impls return `Ok(…)`; fallible `TryFrom`
698	    impls forward their error.
699	  - `fn run(ctx: &Context, params: &Self::Params)
700	    -> Result<(), Box<dyn Error>>`
701	  - `fn suppress_banner(&self) -> bool { false }` — `chid` /
702	    `desc` / `list` / `show` override.
703	  - `fn is_detached_exec(&self) -> bool { false }` —
704	    `finalize` overrides.
705	- Free helper `dispatch<S: Subcommand>(args: &S, log: …)
706	  -> ExitCode` wraps the per-arm body.
707	- `main.rs` match arms collapse to one line each; the
708	  banner / detached-exec peek logic moves behind the trait.
709	
710	Shape is subject to refinement once `-1` exercises it on
711	`chid`.
712	
713	### Ladder
714	
715	- 0.50.0-0 plan + version bump + this section + todo ladder
716	  + linkme/inventory todos (current)
717	- 0.50.0-1 add `subcommand.rs` (trait) + port `chid`
718	  (worked example)
719	- 0.50.0-2..N port remaining 11 subcommands. Grouping
720	  decided per substep; candidate split:
721	  - `From` (total): `validate_desc`, `fix_desc`, `clone`,
722	    `init`, `symlink`, `sync`, `push`
723	  - `TryFrom` (fallible): `desc`, `list`, `show`, `finalize`
724	- 0.50.0-K `main.rs` dispatch rework — drop the per-arm
725	  match boilerplate; move banner / detached-exec peeks
726	  behind the trait.
727	- 0.50.0 close-out — drop suffix, todo→Done.
728	
729	### Per-step evaluation
730	
731	Effectiveness of the trait approach is evaluated after each
732	substep. Possible outcomes at any step boundary:
733	
734	- Continue as planned.
735	- Significantly modify the trait shape (e.g. swap
736	  `into_params`/`run` for a single `dispatch` method on the
737	  trait; absorb the `Args` type as an associated type instead
738	  of `impl Subcommand for ArgsType`; etc.) — recorded in a
739	  new chores subsection at the next step.
740	- Abandon: revert the cycle to a non-trait baseline (close
741	  the cycle as a no-op with a chores `### Outcome` note
742	  capturing what didn't fit). The version-bump and todo
743	  entries land in Done as the record of "tried, didn't
744	  ship."
745	
746	This is part of the cycle's contract, not an exceptional
747	exit path — the trait sweep is the lower-risk reading of
748	"reduce per-arm boilerplate" and `linkme` / `inventory` are
749	the higher-leverage / higher-cost alternatives queued as
750	follow-up todos.
751	
752	### Why not linkme / inventory now
753	
754	Both eliminate the `Commands` enum entirely (link-time
755	distributed slice or runtime registry). They'd cut
756	per-subcommand touchpoints from 3 (mod decl + enum variant +
757	match arm) to 1 (registration). Costs: compile-time
758	exhaustiveness check goes away (missing registration =
759	runtime gap); help-output ordering becomes link-order
760	unless sorted; either is macro-magic dependency. The trait
761	sweep gets every match arm to one line with none of those
762	costs; linkme + inventory are queued as separate Todo items
763	to revisit if the per-arm cost ever feels burdensome.
764	
765	### Per-substep contract
766	
767	Per `notes/substep-protocol.md`: `cargo fmt` / `clippy
768	--all-targets -- -D warnings` / `test` / `install --path .
769	--locked` + retest before each commit; bump `Cargo.toml` at
770	sub-step start; flip todo ladder markers; pair commits
771	across both repos with ochid trailers.
772	
773	## refactor: SubcommandRunner trait + chid (0.50.0-1)
774	
775	Commits: [[14]]
776	
777	Worked example for the 0.50.0 cycle: introduce the
778	`SubcommandRunner` trait, validate its shape on `chid`, and
779	extract `main.rs`'s session-chrome into a global `sb_ide`
780	function consumed both by the trait's default `dispatch`
781	(for ported commands) and from `main` directly (for
782	unported arms). The Chid arm in `main` reaches its target
783	one-liner — `Commands::Chid(args) => args.dispatch(cli.log),`
784	— with chid's banner suppression riding on a new
785	`suppress_banner` field in `ChidParams` that the trait's
786	default `dispatch` reads via
787	`SubcommandRunner::suppress_banner(&params)`.
788	
789	Three material design calls landed at this substep's
790	evaluation gate (full discussion below): the trait was
791	renamed from `Subcommand` to `SubcommandRunner` to sidestep
792	a `clap::Subcommand` collision; the session-chrome block
793	(banner + `surface_previous_failures`) was extracted into a
794	global `pub fn sb_ide` in `main.rs`; and the trait's
795	`suppress_banner` / `is_detached_exec` peeks were *kept* on
796	the trait — default `false`, signature `(_params:
797	&Self::Params)` — with the chrome data living on each
798	command's `Params` (e.g. `ChidParams::suppress_banner`) so

file.content:
## chore: open Subcommand trait sweep (0.50.0-0)

Commits: [[13]]

Multi-step. The 12 subcommand match arms in `main.rs` repeat
the same `Context::load` + `try_from(&args)` + `run_command`
boilerplate — ~12 lines per arm. This cycle introduces a
`Subcommand` trait so each arm collapses to a single
`dispatch::<T>(args, cli.log)` line while keeping the
`Commands` enum as the dispatch source of truth (compile-time
exhaustiveness preserved). Plan-only opener; the trait + first
worked-example port land in `-1`.

### Trait shape (initial sketch)

- `Subcommand` trait on each subcommand's `Args` type:
  - `type Params`
  - `fn into_params(&self) -> Result<Self::Params, String>`
    — total `From` impls return `Ok(…)`; fallible `TryFrom`
    impls forward their error.
  - `fn run(ctx: &Context, params: &Self::Params)
    -> Result<(), Box<dyn Error>>`
  - `fn suppress_banner(&self) -> bool { false }` — `chid` /
    `desc` / `list` / `show` override.
  - `fn is_detached_exec(&self) -> bool { false }` —
    `finalize` overrides.
- Free helper `dispatch<S: Subcommand>(args: &S, log: …)
  -> ExitCode` wraps the per-arm body.
- `main.rs` match arms collapse to one line each; the
  banner / detached-exec peek logic moves behind the trait.

Shape is subject to refinement once `-1` exercises it on
`chid`.

### Ladder

- 0.50.0-0 plan + version bump + this section + todo ladder
  + linkme/inventory todos (current)
- 0.50.0-1 add `subcommand.rs` (trait) + port `chid`
  (worked example)
- 0.50.0-2..N port remaining 11 subcommands. Grouping
  decided per substep; candidate split:
  - `From` (total): `validate_desc`, `fix_desc`, `clone`,
    `init`, `symlink`, `sync`, `push`
  - `TryFrom` (fallible): `desc`, `list`, `show`, `finalize`
- 0.50.0-K `main.rs` dispatch rework — drop the per-arm
  match boilerplate; move banner / detached-exec peeks
  behind the trait.
- 0.50.0 close-out — drop suffix, todo→Done.

### Per-step evaluation

Effectiveness of the trait approach is evaluated after each
substep. Possible outcomes at any step boundary:

- Continue as planned.
- Significantly modify the trait shape (e.g. swap
  `into_params`/`run` for a single `dispatch` method on the
  trait; absorb the `Args` type as an associated type instead
  of `impl Subcommand for ArgsType`; etc.) — recorded in a
  new chores subsection at the next step.
- Abandon: revert the cycle to a non-trait baseline (close
  the cycle as a no-op with a chores `### Outcome` note
  capturing what didn't fit). The version-bump and todo
  entries land in Done as the record of "tried, didn't
  ship."

This is part of the cycle's contract, not an exceptional
exit path — the trait sweep is the lower-risk reading of
"reduce per-arm boilerplate" and `linkme` / `inventory` are
the higher-leverage / higher-cost alternatives queued as
follow-up todos.

### Why not linkme / inventory now

Both eliminate the `Commands` enum entirely (link-time
distributed slice or runtime registry). They'd cut
per-subcommand touchpoints from 3 (mod decl + enum variant +
match arm) to 1 (registration). Costs: compile-time
exhaustiveness check goes away (missing registration =
runtime gap); help-output ordering becomes link-order
unless sorted; either is macro-magic dependency. The trait
sweep gets every match arm to one line with none of those
costs; linkme + inventory are queued as separate Todo items
to revisit if the per-arm cost ever feels burdensome.

### Per-substep contract

Per `notes/substep-protocol.md`: `cargo fmt` / `clippy
--all-targets -- -D warnings` / `test` / `install --path .
--locked` + retest before each commit; bump `Cargo.toml` at
sub-step start; flip todo ladder markers; pair commits
across both repos with ochid trailers.

## refactor: SubcommandRunner trait + chid (0.50.0-1)

Commits: [[14]]

Worked example for the 0.50.0 cycle: introduce the
`SubcommandRunner` trait, validate its shape on `chid`, and
extract `main.rs`'s session-chrome into a global `sb_ide`
function consumed both by the trait's default `dispatch`
(for ported commands) and from `main` directly (for
unported arms). The Chid arm in `main` reaches its target
one-liner — `Commands::Chid(args) => args.dispatch(cli.log),`
— with chid's banner suppression riding on a new
`suppress_banner` field in `ChidParams` that the trait's
default `dispatch` reads via
`SubcommandRunner::suppress_banner(&params)`.

Three material design calls landed at this substep's
evaluation gate (full discussion below): the trait was
renamed from `Subcommand` to `SubcommandRunner` to sidestep
a `clap::Subcommand` collision; the session-chrome block
(banner + `surface_previous_failures`) was extracted into a
global `pub fn sb_ide` in `main.rs`; and the trait's
`suppress_banner` / `is_detached_exec` peeks were *kept* on
the trait — default `false`, signature `(_params:
&Self::Params)` — with the chrome data living on each
command's `Params` (e.g. `ChidParams::suppress_banner`) so

1050 notes/chores-10.md

## chore: --exec doc + matches! → match (0.50.0-1.2)

Inter-substep tidy: a small readability rewrite in
`main.rs` and a doc expansion on `FinalizeArgs::exec` so
the `--detach` / `--exec` handshake is spelled out at
the field that implements it.

- `src/main.rs`: `let is_detached_exec = matches!(cli.command,
  Commands::Finalize(ref f) if f.exec)` → explicit
  two-arm `match`. Semantically identical (both expand to
  the same thing); the trailing-`if`-guard inside
  `matches!` was found weird enough to be worth replacing
  at this site.
- `src/finalize.rs`: `FinalizeArgs::exec` doc expanded
  from the one-liner "Internal: run exec path (used by
  detach)" to a paragraph anchored on the sibling `pub
  detach` field — when `--detach` is passed the parent
  `fork`s and re-execs `vc-x1 finalize --exec …`, the
  child sees `exec: true` and routes through
  `finalize_exec()` for the background work — plus a
  second paragraph spelling out why `hide = true` is set.

## chore: dicom-rs gotchas → todo (0.50.0-1.1)

Commits: [[15]]

Inter-substep tidy: record two surfacings from the
manual por→dual conversion of `../dicom-rs`
(2026-05-14) — the user not only converted a por
workspace by hand but also named the bot side `.bot`
(rather than `.claude`), exercising two
not-yet-covered seams.

**Gotcha 1 — por → dual is arduous.** The bundled
`notes/todo.md` "por/dual parity + bidirectional
conversion" entry was split: `por → dual` is promoted
to its own higher-priority item; the remaining entry
covers parity + the `dual → por` (detach) direction.
The manual exercise was incoherent enough that the user
plans to redo it, which makes a routine subcommand
worth surfacing separately from the broader parity
goal. Both items continue to point at
`notes/por-dual-parity.md`; the design stub already
covers both directions and needs no edit.

**Gotcha 2 — `.claude` hardcoded in diagnostics.**
`vc-x1 finalize --repo .bot` honored `--repo`
throughout the actual operations (squash, bookmark set,
push to origin all ran against `.bot/`), but the
`bm-track` enter/exit lines still emitted
`.claude(main)=no-jj` — the diagnostic label has
`.claude` baked into the string. Cosmetic today (output
text only), but load-bearing once bot-repo name
becomes configurable.

- `notes/todo.md`: new "**`por → dual` conversion.**"
  entry inserted above the (now trimmed) parity item;
  trimmed item retitled "**por/dual parity + `dual →
  por` conversion.**" — wording adjusted to reflect
  the remaining scope; both reference `[[4]]`. New
  "**Audit hardcoded `.claude` in diagnostics /
  logging.**" entry inserted just below them.

## refactor: hoist Context::load (0.50.0-2)

`Context::load` was invoked in every match arm — once
in `dispatch` for the ported `Chid` arm, and inline at
the head of each of the 10 unported arms. The body is
identical, the result is identical, only one arm runs
per invocation. Hoist the load to a single site at the
top of the match in `main`, change
`SubcommandRunner::dispatch` to take `&Context` instead
of building one itself, and let the unported arms read
the same `&ctx`. Net `-63` lines in `main.rs`.

The trait shape change is small but load-bearing for
the rest of the cycle — every future port arrives at the
settled `dispatch(&ctx)` shape rather than the
`dispatch(cli.log)` shape. Splitting the hoist out as
its own substep keeps the per-port commits (starting
with `desc` at `-3`) reviewable in isolation: each one
becomes "add `suppress_banner` + impl `SubcommandRunner`
+ collapse the arm" with no infrastructure churn mixed
in.

- `src/main.rs`: `Context::load` lifted out of each
  match arm into a single block above the `match
  cli.command`; 10 inline blocks deleted.
- `src/subcommand.rs`: `dispatch(&self, log:
  Option<PathBuf>)` → `dispatch(&self, ctx: &Context)`;
  the inline `Context::load` inside dispatch deleted.
  Module + item doc-comments updated for the new
  signature (`args.dispatch(&ctx)`, "~11 lines"
  encapsulated, "Context is loaded once in main and
  passed in by reference"). Unused `use
  std::path::PathBuf` deleted.

file.content:
"**Audit hardcoded `.claude` in diagnostics /
  logging.**" entry inserted just below them.

## refactor: hoist Context::load (0.50.0-2)

`Context::load` was invoked in every match arm — once
in `dispatch` for the ported `Chid` arm, and inline at
the head of each of the 10 unported arms. The body is
identical, the result is identical, only one arm runs
per invocation. Hoist the load to a single site at the
top of the match in `main`, change
`SubcommandRunner::dispatch` to take `&Context` instead
of building one itself, and let the unported arms read
the same `&ctx`. Net `-63` lines in `main.rs`.

The trait shape change is small but load-bearing for
the rest of the cycle — every future port arrives at the
settled `dispatch(&ctx)` shape rather than the
`dispatch(cli.log)` shape. Splitting the hoist out as
its own substep keeps the per-port commits (starting
with `desc` at `-3`) reviewable in isolation: each one
becomes "add `suppress_banner` + impl `SubcommandRunner`
+ collapse the arm" with no infrastructure churn mixed
in.

- `src/main.rs`: `Context::load` lifted out of each
  match arm into a single block above the `match
  cli.command`; 10 inline blocks deleted.
- `src/subcommand.rs`: `dispatch(&self, log:
  Option<PathBuf>)` → `dispatch(&self, ctx: &Context)`;
  the inline `Context::load` inside dispatch deleted.
  Module + item doc-comments updated for the new
  signature (`args.dispatch(&ctx)`, "~11 lines"
  encapsulated, "Context is loaded once in main and
  passed in by reference"). Unused `use
  std::path::PathBuf` deleted.

## chore: --exec doc + matches! → match (0.50.0-1.2)

Commits: [[16]]

Inter-substep tidy: a small readability rewrite in
`main.rs` and a doc expansion on `FinalizeArgs::exec` so
the `--detach` / `--exec` handshake is spelled out at
the field that implements it.

- `src/main.rs`: `let is_detached_exec = matches!(cli.command,
  Commands::Finalize(ref f) if f.exec)` → explicit
  two-arm `match`. Semantically identical (both expand to
  the same thing); the trailing-`if`-guard inside
  `matches!` was found weird enough to be worth replacing
  at this site.
- `src/finalize.rs`: `FinalizeArgs::exec` doc expanded
  from the one-liner "Internal: run exec path (used by
  detach)" to a paragraph anchored on the sibling `pub
  detach` field — when `--detach` is passed the parent
  `fork`s and re-execs `vc-x1 finalize --exec …`, the
  child sees `exec: true` and routes through
  `finalize_exec()` for the background work — plus a
  second paragraph spelling out why `hide = true` is set.

# References

content.text:
Work complete. Summary of changes:

**App repo:**
- `Cargo.toml`: `0.50.0-1.2` → `0.50.0-2`
- `src/main.rs`: `Context::load` hoisted out of every match arm into a single block above the match; 10 inline blocks deleted (net −63 lines)
- `src/subcommand.rs`: `dispatch(&self, log: Option<PathBuf>)` → `dispatch(&self, ctx: &Context)`; inline `Context::load` removed; module + item docs updated; unused `PathBuf` import dropped
- `notes/chores-10.md`: backfilled `Commits: [[16]]` on the 0.50.0-1.2 section + added [16] ref; new `## refactor: hoist Context::load (0.50.0-2)` section
- `notes/todo.md`: ladder updated — `0.50.0-2 hoist Context::load + dispatch(&Context) (current)` + new `0.50.0-3..N` placeholder

**Cargo cycle:** fmt ✓ clippy --all-targets -D warnings ✓ test ✓ install --locked ✓ smoke (chid ported + desc unported both work with `-L`) ✓

Proposed commit title and body for `vc-x1 push main`:

```
refactor: hoist Context::load (0.50.0-2)
```

```
Lift `Context::load` out of every per-arm body in
`main.rs` into a single site above the match; change
`SubcommandRunner::dispatch` to take `&Context` instead
of building one itself. Net -63 lines in `main.rs`.

Splitting the hoist out as its own substep keeps the
upcoming per-port commits (starting with `desc` at
`-3`) reviewable in isolation — each one becomes "add
`suppress_banner` + impl `SubcommandRunner` + collapse
the arm" with no infrastructure churn mixed in. See
`notes/chores-10.md > refactor: hoist Context::load
(0.50.0-2)` for the design discussion.

- Cargo.toml: 0.50.0-1.2 → 0.50.0-2.
- src/main.rs: Context::load lifted into a single
  block above `match cli.command`; 10 inline blocks
  deleted.
- src/subcommand.rs: dispatch signature changed to
  `dispatch(&self, ctx: &Context)`; inline
  Context::load deleted; module/item doc-comments
  updated for the new signature; unused
  `std::path::PathBuf` import deleted.
- notes/chores-10.md: backfilled Commits: [[16]] on
  the 0.50.0-1.2 section; new section "refactor:
  hoist Context::load (0.50.0-2)".
- notes/todo.md: ladder updated — 0.50.0-2 hoist
  (current); 0.50.0-3..N placeholder added.
```

Please review. On approval I'll run `vc-x1 push main`.

message.content: LG, and the commit description is ok, but I'd like to shorten it and remove the "sub-statement" following the semi-colon in each, I don't think the extra detail is necessary. Thoughts?

content.text:
Agreed — the post-semicolon details are mostly consequences of the main change in each bullet. For `subcommand.rs`, dropping the sub-statements gives "dispatch signature changed to `dispatch(&self, ctx: &Context)`" which is the load-bearing change; the inline-`Context::load` removal and doc updates are downstream of that and the diff itself shows them.

Trimmed body:

```
Lift `Context::load` out of every per-arm body in

`SubcommandRunner::dispatch` to take `&Context` instead
of building one itself. Net -63 lines in `main.rs`.

Splitting the hoist out as its own substep keeps the
upcoming per-port commits (starting with `desc` at
`-3`) reviewable in isolation — each one becomes "add
`suppress_banner` + impl `SubcommandRunner` + collapse
the arm" with no infrastructure churn mixed in. See
`notes/chores-10.md > refactor: hoist Context::load
(0.50.0-2)` for the design discussion.

- Cargo.toml: 0.50.0-1.2 → 0.50.0-2.
- src/main.rs: Context::load lifted into a single
  block above `match cli.command`.
- src/subcommand.rs: dispatch signature changed to
  `dispatch(&self, ctx: &Context)`.
- notes/chores-10.md: backfilled Commits: [[16]] on
  the 0.50.0-1.2 section.
- notes/todo.md: ladder updated — 0.50.0-2 hoist
  (current).
```

OK to push with this body?

message.content: yes push it

## refactor: desc → SubcommandRunner (0.50.0-3)

First port off the `chid` worked example: `desc` lands
on the trait-based `dispatch` shape settled in `-2`.
The arm in `main.rs` collapses to
`Commands::Desc(args) => args.dispatch(&ctx),`, and
`desc` joins `chid` in the trait-owned banner-suppression
path (so `desc -L` no longer needs a special-case match
arm at the top of `main`).

Mechanical, no design calls. The shape mirrors `chid`
(0.50.0-1) exactly: add a `suppress_banner: bool` to
`DescParams`, set it in `TryFrom<&DescArgs>` from
`a.common.no_label`, and implement `SubcommandRunner for
DescArgs` with `to_params` / `run` / `suppress_banner`
overrides. The `desc` module doc-comment is unchanged —
`chid`'s isn't either, so the trait impl is left visible
inside the file rather than re-advertised at the top.

- `Cargo.toml`: `0.50.0-2` → `0.50.0-3`.
- `src/desc.rs`: `SubcommandRunner for DescArgs` impl
  added; `suppress_banner: bool` field added to
  `DescParams`; `TryFrom<&DescArgs>` updated to copy
  `a.common.no_label` into it.
- `src/main.rs`: `Commands::Desc(...)` arm collapsed
  to `args.dispatch(&ctx)`; `Commands::Desc` line
  dropped from the `suppress_banner` match; doc
  comment over that match updated to reflect that
  `desc` is now trait-owned alongside `chid`.
- `notes/chores-10.md`: backfilled `Commits: [[17]]`
  on the 0.50.0-2 section.
- `notes/todo.md`: ladder updated — flip 0.50.0-2 →
  `(done)` (absorbing the missed flip from the prior
  commit); add `0.50.0-3 port desc (current)`;
  placeholder updated to "remaining 10 subcommands".

## refactor: list → SubcommandRunner (0.50.0-4)

Second mechanical port off the trait shape settled in
`-2`: `list` follows `desc` (`-3`) onto
`SubcommandRunner::dispatch`. The arm in `main.rs`
collapses to `Commands::List(args) => args.dispatch(&ctx),`,
and `list -L` joins `chid -L` / `desc -L` in the
trait-owned banner-suppression path. Only `show` remains
in the top-level `suppress_banner` match.

Same shape as the `desc` port. `ListParams` already
carried a non-`CommonParams` field (`width`) — the
new `suppress_banner: bool` simply joins it; nothing
about the trait shape changes to accommodate the
"`Params` has extra fields" case.

- `Cargo.toml`: `0.50.0-3` → `0.50.0-4`.
- `src/list.rs`: `SubcommandRunner for ListArgs` impl
  added; `suppress_banner: bool` field on `ListParams`.
- `src/main.rs`: `List` arm collapsed to
  `args.dispatch(&ctx)`; `Commands::List` line dropped
  from the `suppress_banner` match; doc comment updated
  to reflect that `list` is now trait-owned alongside
  `chid` / `desc`.

message.content: This is pretty mechanical, any reason not to do the remainder in one commit?

content.text:
No real reason to keep splitting — the remaining 9 ports are the same edit pattern × 9, and the diff would be boring but reviewable in one pass. The only nuance is that **finalize** also needs `is_detached_exec` plumbed through the trait (the trait already has the override slot, just no implementor uses it yet), but that's still mechanical.

My suggested shape:

- **0.50.0-5** — all 9 remaining ports + K cleanup (delete the top-level `is_detached_exec` / `suppress_banner` matches and the `sb_ide()` call from `main`, since every arm now dispatches through the trait).
- **0.50.0** (no suffix) — cycle close-out: move todo entry to Done, add `## chore: close Subcommand trait sweep` section.

That collapses 9 mechanical substeps + K + close-out into 2 commits. The main tradeoff vs. continuing one-at-a-time is bisect granularity — if one of the 9 ports introduces a regression, the failing commit names "all 9 ports + K" rather than a single subcommand. But ports are independent and the test suite covers each subcommand, so that risk is small.

OK to proceed with that shape, or do you want to keep per-port?

1	//! `vc-x1 clone` — clone a repo (URL or local path) into a workspace.
2	//!
3	//! - `--scope=code,bot` (default): dual-repo layout — clones code,
4	//!   derives bot source (`<source>.claude`), clones bot into
5	//!   `<target>/.claude`, creates the Claude Code symlink. Both
6	//!   sides must succeed.
7	//! - `--scope=por`: single repo into `<target>`. No `.claude/`,
8	//!   no symlink.
9	//!
10	//! TARGET shapes (all routed through `parse_target`): URL,
11	//! `owner/name` shorthand, or a local path (`./X`, `/X`, `~/X`,
12	//! `.`, `..`). Path-form is symmetric with `git clone /local/bare.git`
13	//! — useful for fixtures and CI scratch dirs.
14	//!
15	//! `clone_one` and `clone_dual` are `pub(crate)` so init's `-3`
16	//! reshape can reuse them for the "URL exists → clone" preflight
17	//! path (per `notes/chores-08.md > init + clone redesign`).
18	
19	use std::path::Path;
20	
21	use clap::Args;
22	use log::info;
23	
24	use crate::common::run;
25	use crate::context::Context;
26	use crate::options_flags::scope::{ScopeKind, parse_scope_kind};
27	use crate::symlink;
28	use crate::url::{Target, derive_name, derive_session_url, parse_target, resolve_url};
29	
30	/// CLI args for `vc-x1 clone`.

1	//! The `symlink` subcommand: manage the
2	//! `~/.claude/projects/<encoded-cwd>` symlink that points Claude
3	//! Code's per-project session directory at this workspace's
4	//! `.claude` repo.
5	//!
6	//! Also home to the shared `SymLink` planner (`new` / `with_meta`
7	//! / `create`), `encode_path`, and the `install` helper that
8	//! `vc-x1 init` / `clone` reuse for dual-scope runs.
9	
10	use std::path::{Path, PathBuf};
11	
12	use clap::Args;
13	use log::{debug, info};
14	
15	use crate::context::Context;
16	
17	/// What action the symlink operation needs to take.
18	#[derive(Debug, PartialEq, Eq)]
19	pub enum SymlinkAction {
20	    /// Nothing exists at the symlink path — create it.
21	    Create,
22	    /// An existing symlink points somewhere else — caller must decide whether to replace.
23	    Replace { current_target: PathBuf },
24	    /// The symlink already points to the correct target — nothing to do.
25	    AlreadyCorrect,
26	}
27	
28	/// A planned symlink operation.
29	#[derive(Debug)]
30	pub struct SymLink {
31	    /// Absolute path to the target directory (what the symlink points to).
32	    pub abs_target: PathBuf,
33	    /// Full path of the symlink to create/replace.
34	    pub symlink_path: PathBuf,
35	    /// What action is needed.
36	    pub action: SymlinkAction,
37	}
38	
39	/// Encode a path the way Claude Code does: replace `/` and `.` with `-`.
40	pub fn encode_path(path: &str) -> String {

1	//! The `sync` subcommand: fetch + classify + (optionally) rebase /
2	//! fast-forward a workspace's repos against their remotes.
3	//!
4	//! Default is `--check` (fetch + report, error if any repo needs
5	//! action); `--no-check` applies. On any failure every repo is
6	//! reverted to its pre-sync op via `jj op restore`.
7	//!
8	//! `current_op_id` / `op_restore` are `pub(crate)` so `push` reuses
9	//! the same snapshot-and-restore pattern.
10	
11	use std::path::{Path, PathBuf};
12	use std::process::Command;
13	
14	use clap::Args;
15	use log::{LevelFilter, debug, info, warn};
16	
17	use crate::common::{default_scope, find_workspace_root, run, scope_to_repos};
18	use crate::context::Context;
19	use crate::scope::{Scope, parse_scope};
20	
21	/// Fetch and sync a set of repos to their remotes.
22	///
23	/// Default is `--check`: fetch + classify + report, **error** if any
24	/// repo is `behind` or `diverged`. Pass `--no-check` to actually
25	/// rebase / fast-forward. On any failure the starting state of every
26	/// repo is restored via `jj op restore`.
27	///
28	/// Scripts and automation should pass `--check` or `--no-check`
29	/// explicitly rather than relying on the default — defaults can shift,
30	/// explicit flags lock in the contract.

## refactor: port 8 → SubcommandRunner (0.50.0-5)

Bulk port: the 8 subcommands that don't need the
`is_detached_exec` peek — `show` (TryFrom +
suppress_banner), and the 7 total-`From` commands
(`validate_desc`, `fix_desc`, `clone`, `init`,
`symlink`, `sync`, `push`). Each arm in `main.rs`
collapses to `args.dispatch(&ctx)`. After this commit,
`finalize` is the only remaining unported arm; it
lands separately in `-6` because its
`is_detached_exec=true` plumbing is the only
non-mechanical bit left.

The top-level `suppress_banner` match in `main.rs` is
gone (show was its last consumer). The
`is_detached_exec` match remains — `finalize`'s arm
still reads it; the `bm_track` enter/exit gates also
still consult it. Both will fold into the trait when
finalize ports in `-6`.

Shape per command:

- **`show`** (TryFrom): same as `desc` / `list` —
  added `suppress_banner: bool` to `ShowParams`, set
  from `a.common.no_label` in `TryFrom`, impl
  `SubcommandRunner` with `to_params` / `run` /
  `suppress_banner` overrides.
- **7 `From` commands**: no `suppress_banner` flag
  needed (none of them have `-L`). `to_params`
  returns `Ok(XxxParams::from(self))`; `run`
  forwards to the existing op function. Trait
  defaults cover `suppress_banner` / `is_detached_exec`
  (both `false`).

- `Cargo.toml`: `0.50.0-4` → `0.50.0-5`.
- `src/show.rs`: `SubcommandRunner for ShowArgs` impl
  added; `suppress_banner` field on `ShowParams`.
- `src/validate_desc.rs` / `src/fix_desc.rs` /
  `src/clone.rs` / `src/init.rs` / `src/symlink.rs` /
  `src/sync.rs` / `src/push.rs`: `SubcommandRunner`
  impl added per file.
- `src/main.rs`: 8 arms collapsed to
  `args.dispatch(&ctx)`; `let suppress_banner = …`
  match deleted (no remaining consumer); finalize's
  `sb_ide(suppress_banner, …)` → `sb_ide(false, …)`.
- `notes/chores-10.md`: backfilled `Commits: [[19]]`
  on the 0.50.0-4 section.
- `notes/todo.md`: ladder updated — 0.50.0-4 →
  `(done)`; add `0.50.0-5 port 8 subcommands (current)`
  and `0.50.0-6 port finalize` entry.

# References

attachment.snippet:
1	//! The `sync` subcommand: fetch + classify + (optionally) rebase /
2	//! fast-forward a workspace's repos against their remotes.
3	//!
4	//! Default is `--check` (fetch + report, error if any repo needs
5	//! action); `--no-check` applies. On any failure every repo is
6	//! reverted to its pre-sync op via `jj op restore`.
7	//!
8	//! `current_op_id` / `op_restore` are `pub(crate)` so `push` reuses
9	//! the same snapshot-and-restore pattern.
10	
11	use std::path::{Path, PathBuf};
12	use std::process::Command;
13	
14	use clap::Args;
15	use log::{LevelFilter, debug, info, warn};
16	
17	use crate::common::{default_scope, find_workspace_root, run, scope_to_repos};
18	use crate::context::Context;
19	use crate::scope::{Scope, parse_scope};
20	use crate::subcommand::SubcommandRunner;
21	
22	/// Fetch and sync a set of repos to their remotes.
23	///
24	/// Default is `--check`: fetch + classify + report, **error** if any
25	/// repo is `behind` or `diverged`. Pass `--no-check` to actually
26	/// rebase / fast-forward. On any failure the starting state of every
27	/// repo is restored via `jj op restore`.
28	///
29	/// Scripts and automation should pass `--check` or `--no-check`
30	/// explicitly rather than relying on the default — defaults can shift,
31	/// explicit flags lock in the contract.
32	///
33	/// Repo set is resolved from `--scope`:
34	///
35	/// - Explicit `--scope=code|bot|code,bot|<path>` — keyword forms
36	///   resolve via the project root's `.vc-config.toml`; the path
37	///   form syncs that one repo (single-repo mode).
38	/// - No `--scope` — workspace-default scope:
39	///   - dual workspace (`.vc-config.toml` with `other-repo`) → `code,bot`
40	///   - single-repo workspace (`.vc-config.toml`, no `other-repo`) → `code`
41	///   - POR (no `.vc-config.toml`) → cwd as a single-repo target
42	#[derive(Args, Debug)]
43	pub struct SyncArgs {
44	    /// Verify only — fetch + classify; error if any repo needs action.
45	    /// This is the default; pass explicitly in scripts.
46	    #[arg(long, conflicts_with = "no_check")]
47	    pub check: bool,
48	
49	    /// Apply — fetch + classify, then rebase / fast-forward as needed.
50	    #[arg(long, conflicts_with = "check")]
51	    pub no_check: bool,
52	
53	    /// Suppress all informational output (exit code signals result)
54	    #[arg(short, long)]
55	    pub quiet: bool,
56	
57	    /// Bookmark to sync in each repo
58	    #[arg(long, default_value = "main")]
59	    pub bookmark: String,
60	
61	    /// Remote to sync against
62	    #[arg(long, default_value = "origin")]
63	    pub remote: String,
64	
65	    /// Which repo(s) of the workspace to sync.
66	    ///
67	    /// `SCOPE=code|bot|code,bot|<path>`:
68	    ///
69	    /// - `code` — sync only the app repo.
70	    /// - `bot` — sync only the bot repo (errors if no bot repo
71	    ///   is configured).
72	    /// - `code,bot` — sync both repos.
73	    /// - `<path>` (`./X`, `/X`, `~/X`) — single-repo mode; sync
74	    ///   only that repo, ignoring `.vc-config.toml`.
75	    ///
76	    /// Default depends on workspace state: dual workspace →
77	    /// `code,bot`; single-repo workspace → `code`; POR → cwd.
78	    #[arg(
79	        short = 's',
80	        long,
81	        value_name = "SCOPE",
82	        value_parser = parse_scope,
83	        verbatim_doc_comment
84	    )]
85	    pub scope: Option<Scope>,
86	}
87	
88	/// Inputs to the sync op, flat, owned, clap-free.
89	///
90	/// - `quiet`: `-q` / `--quiet` — clamp output to `Warn` for the run.
91	/// - `bookmark`: bookmark to sync in each repo (default `main`).
92	/// - `remote`: remote to sync against (default `origin`).
93	/// - `no_check`: `--no-check` — actually rebase / fast-forward
94	///   (absent ⇒ check mode: fetch + report only). The `--check`
95	///   flag is the explicit form of the default and carries no
96	///   value of its own, so it isn't mirrored here.
97	/// - `scope`: `--scope` parsed (None ⇒ resolve via the
98	///   workspace-default scope at run time).
99	pub struct SyncParams {
100	    pub quiet: bool,
101	    pub bookmark: String,
102	    pub remote: String,
103	    pub no_check: bool,
104	    pub scope: Option<Scope>,
105	}
106	
107	impl From<&SyncArgs> for SyncParams {
108	    /// Convert clap-derived `SyncArgs` into the flat `SyncParams`.
109	    /// `--check` is dropped — it's the explicit form of the
110	    /// default; the op only ever consults `no_check`.
111	    fn from(a: &SyncArgs) -> Self {
112	        Self {
113	            quiet: a.quiet,
114	            bookmark: a.bookmark.clone(),
115	            remote: a.remote.clone(),
116	            no_check: a.no_check,
117	            scope: a.scope.clone(),
118	        }
119	    }
120	}
121	
122	impl SubcommandRunner for SyncArgs {
123	    type Params = SyncParams;
124	
125	    /// Delegate to the existing `From<&SyncArgs>` impl above
126	    /// (total — never fails).
127	    fn to_params(&self) -> Result<Self::Params, String> {
128	        Ok(SyncParams::from(self))
129	    }
130	
131	    /// Run the existing `sync` op.
132	    fn run(ctx: &Context, params: &Self::Params) -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
133	        sync(ctx, params)
134	    }
135	}
136	
137	/// Resolve `params` into the concrete repo list `sync_repos`
138	/// operates on.
139	///
140	/// Explicit `--scope` wins; otherwise resolves via `default_scope`
141	/// against the discovered workspace root and cwd.
142	fn resolve_params_to_repos(
143	    params: &SyncParams,
144	) -> Result<Vec<PathBuf>, Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
145	    let workspace_root = find_workspace_root();
146	    let cwd = std::env::current_dir()?;
147	    let scope = match &params.scope {
148	        Some(s) => s.clone(),
149	        None => default_scope(workspace_root.as_deref(), &cwd),
150	    };
151	    scope_to_repos(&scope, workspace_root.as_deref())
152	}
153	
154	/// Relationship between a local bookmark and its remote counterpart.
155	#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]
156	pub enum State {
157	    /// Local and remote point at the same commit.
158	    UpToDate,
159	    /// Local is a strict ancestor of remote — fast-forward possible.
160	    Behind { local: String, remote: String },
161	    /// Remote is a strict ancestor of local — nothing to pull in.
162	    Ahead { local: String, remote: String },
163	    /// Neither is an ancestor of the other — needs rebase.
164	    Diverged { local: String, remote: String },
165	    /// The bookmark has no `@<remote>` counterpart.
166	    NoRemote,
167	}
168	
169	/// Per-repo context accumulated between the snapshot and action phases.
170	#[derive(Debug)]
171	struct RepoCtx {
172	    path: PathBuf,
173	    #[allow(dead_code)]
174	    op_id: String,
175	    state: State,
176	}
177	
178	/// CLI entry point for the `sync` subcommand.
179	///
180	/// Thin wrapper over `sync_repos` that resolves `--scope` into a
181	/// concrete repo list (falling back to the workspace-default
182	/// scope) and forwards the rest. Tests call `sync_repos` directly
183	/// with absolute fixture paths. `ctx` is unused today — present
184	/// for the uniform subcommand-layer signature.
185	///
186	/// When `--quiet` is set, the global log filter is temporarily
187	/// clamped to `Warn` for the duration of the call and restored on
188	/// return, so `info!` calls throughout sync (plus any
189	/// subprocess-stderr routed through `common::run`) go dark. Errors
190	/// still surface at `Warn` / `Error` so script callers don't lose
191	/// diagnostics.
192	pub fn sync(_ctx: &Context, params: &SyncParams) -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
193	    let repos = resolve_params_to_repos(params)?;
194	    if params.quiet {
195	        let prev = log::max_level();
196	        log::set_max_level(LevelFilter::Warn);
197	        let result = sync_repos(&repos, params);
198	        log::set_max_level(prev);
199	        result
200	    } else {
201	        sync_repos(&repos, params)
202	    }
203	}
204	
205	/// Sync the given repos against their remotes.
206	///
207	/// Orchestrates the full flow: pre-flight clean-check on every repo,
208	/// snapshot each repo's current op id, then hand off to `run_plan` for

content.content:
85	/// Squash, set bookmark, and/or push a jj repo.
86	///
87	/// Designed for the bot to atomically finalize its session repo:
88	/// --detach exits immediately, --delay waits for trailing writes,
89	/// --squash folds them in, --bookmark + --push sends it upstream.
90	/// Every flag is opt-in. See README.md for details.
91	#[derive(Args, Debug)]
92	pub struct FinalizeArgs {
93	    /// Path to jj repo
94	    #[arg(long, default_value = ".")]
95	    pub repo: PathBuf,
96	
97	    #[command(flatten)]
98	    pub squash: SquashOption,
99	
100	    /// Seconds to wait before squashing
101	    #[arg(long, default_value_t = 10.0)]
102	    pub delay: f64,
103	
104	    /// Existing bookmark to push to remote
105	    #[arg(long, value_name = "BOOKMARK")]
106	    pub push: Option<String>,
107	
108	    /// Detach and run in the background
109	    #[arg(long)]
110	    pub detach: bool,
111	
112	    /// The above `pub detach` is true when `--detach` is passed
113	    /// to the `finalize` subcommand. The app then `fork`s and
114	    /// spawns `vc-x1 finalize --exec …`, replacing `--detach`
115	    /// with `--exec`. The child sees `exec: true` and is routed
116	    /// through `finalize_exec()` to do the squash/push work in
117	    /// the background.
118	    ///
119	    /// Marked `hide = true` because it's an internal argument and
120	    /// should not appear in `-h` / `--help` output.
121	    #[arg(long, hide = true)]
122	    pub exec: bool,
123	}
124	
125	/// Per-invocation finalize inputs — the clap-free shape the
126	/// `finalize` op works against. Built from `FinalizeArgs` at the
127	/// binary edge via `TryFrom` (fallible only on repo-path
128	/// canonicalization — `--squash` is already parsed into a
129	/// `SquashSpec` by clap). The `--log` path lives on `Context`,
130	/// not here.
131	#[derive(Debug)]
132	pub struct FinalizeParams {
133	    pub repo: PathBuf,
134	    pub squash: Option<SquashSpec>,
135	    pub delay_secs: f64,
136	    pub bookmark: Option<String>,
137	    pub push: bool,
138	    pub detach: bool,
139	    pub exec: bool,
140	}
141	
142	impl TryFrom<&FinalizeArgs> for FinalizeParams {
143	    type Error = String;
144	
145	    /// Derive `push`/`bookmark` from `--push` and canonicalize
146	    /// `--repo` (so the detached child resolves it regardless of
147	    /// cwd); `--squash` was already parsed by clap.
148	    fn try_from(a: &FinalizeArgs) -> Result<Self, String> {
149	        let repo = std::fs::canonicalize(&a.repo)
150	            .map_err(|e| format!("cannot resolve repo path '{}': {e}", a.repo.display()))?;
151	        Ok(FinalizeParams {
152	            repo,
153	            squash: a.squash.value.clone(),
154	            delay_secs: a.delay,
155	            bookmark: a.push.clone(),
156	            push: a.push.is_some(),
157	            detach: a.detach,
158	            exec: a.exec,
159	        })
160	    }
161	}
162	
163	/// Validate inputs synchronously before detaching.
164	///
165	/// Catches the common failure modes up front so the parent can exit
166	/// with a visible non-zero status — rather than discovering them in
167	/// the detached child where errors only reach the log file.
168	fn preflight(params: &FinalizeParams) -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
169	    debug!("preflight: checking params");
170	    let repo_str = params.repo.to_string_lossy();
171	    let cwd = std::path::Path::new(".");
172	
173	    // Verify squash revsets resolve to something.
174	    if let Some(ref sq) = params.squash {

content.text:
Finalize is straightforward — `FinalizeParams` already has `exec: bool`, so the trait override reads it directly. Quick scoping question before I bump:

The cleanup K mentioned earlier (move `bm_track` enter/exit gates into `dispatch`, delete the top-level `is_detached_exec` match) becomes pure deletion after finalize ports. Two ways to land:

questions.question: How should K cleanup land relative to finalize port?

options.label: Fold K into 0.50.0-6 (finalize + cleanup)
options.description: Once finalize ports, the top-level `is_detached_exec` match exists only to gate `bm_track`. Folding K in means moving `bm_track` enter/exit into `dispatch` and deleting the top-level match — main.rs becomes Context::load + thin dispatch match. Single commit closes the structural work.

options.label: Keep K separate (0.50.0-6 finalize only, then 0.50.0-K)
options.description: 0.50.0-6 ports finalize and leaves bm_track gating in main as-is. Then 0.50.0-K is a small, pure-cleanup commit. Tighter commits; one more push cycle.

## refactor: finalize → SubcommandRunner (0.50.0-6)

Last per-command port: `finalize` joins the other 11
arms on `SubcommandRunner::dispatch`. The
`is_detached_exec` slot on the trait (default `false`,
unused since `-1`) finally gets its implementor —
`FinalizeArgs::is_detached_exec(params)` returns
`params.exec`, which the trait's default `dispatch`
threads into `crate::sb_ide` so the detached
`finalize --exec` child stays silent.

`FinalizeParams` already carried `exec: bool` from
the existing `TryFrom` impl — no new field needed.
The trait override reads it directly.

What lands in this commit:

- The Finalize arm in `main.rs` collapses to one line.
  Every match arm is now `args.dispatch(&ctx)`.
- The `run_command` helper in `main.rs` becomes dead
  (no remaining caller) and is deleted. Clippy
  `-D warnings` would block the cycle otherwise; the
  deletion is the natural consequence of the last
  port.

What does **not** land — deferred to `-K`:

- The top-level `let is_detached_exec = match
  cli.command { Commands::Finalize(ref f) => f.exec,
  _ => false };` block in `main.rs` stays. It gates
  the `bm_track` enter/exit pair which lives outside
  `dispatch`. Moving those calls inside the trait
  (so `main` no longer needs the peek) is `-K`'s job.

After this commit `main.rs`'s exec_code match is
twelve `args.dispatch(&ctx)` lines and nothing else.

- `Cargo.toml`: `0.50.0-5` → `0.50.0-6`.
- `src/finalize.rs`: `SubcommandRunner for FinalizeArgs`
  impl added; `is_detached_exec(params)` override
  returns `params.exec`.
- `src/main.rs`: `Finalize` arm collapsed to
  `args.dispatch(&ctx)`; `fn run_command` deleted
  (now unused).
- `notes/chores-10.md`: backfilled `Commits: [[20]]`
  on the 0.50.0-5 section.
- `notes/todo.md`: 0.50.0-5 → `(done)`; 0.50.0-6
  marked `(current)`.

# References

content.content:
1	//! `SubcommandRunner` trait
2	//!
3	//! The purpose is to simplify invoking a subcommand so `main`
4	//! can invoke any subcommand with one line:
5	//!
6	//! `Commands::Chid(args) => args.dispatch(&ctx),`
7	//!
8	//! Instead of the ~11 lines that the default `dispatch` below
9	//! now encapsulates.
10	//!
11	//! Implementors provide:
12	//!
13	//! - [`SubcommandRunner::to_params`] — build the clap-free
14	//!   `Params` from the clap `Args` (covers both `From` /
15	//!   `TryFrom` shapes uniformly via `Result<_, String>`).
16	//! - [`SubcommandRunner::run`] — the subcommand body.
17	//!
18	//! The default [`SubcommandRunner::dispatch`] builds `Params`
19	//! via `to_params`, runs via `run`, and maps the result to
20	//! `ExitCode`. Errors at any stage log via `error!` and return
21	//! `ExitCode::FAILURE`. `Context` is loaded once in `main` and
22	//! passed in by reference so a single `Context` is shared across
23	//! the (one) match arm that actually runs.
24	//!
25	//! ## See also
26	//!
27	//! - The wider CLI-args / subcommand-layer split this trait
28	//!   formalizes: [`ARCHITECTURE.md > args → Context + Params`][arch].
29	//! - Cycle design, ladder, evaluation gate:
30	//!   [`chores-10.md > 0.50.0-0`][open].
31	//! - Worked example, trait-shape decisions, naming:
32	//!   [`chores-10.md > 0.50.0-1`][port].
33	//! - First implementor: [`crate::chid`].
34	//!
35	//! [arch]: https://github.com/winksaville/vc-x1/blob/main/ARCHITECTURE.md#args--context--params
36	//! [open]: https://github.com/winksaville/vc-x1/blob/main/notes/chores-10.md#chore-open-subcommand-trait-sweep-0500-0
37	//! [port]: https://github.com/winksaville/vc-x1/blob/main/notes/chores-10.md#refactor-subcommandrunner-trait--chid-0500-1
38	
39	use std::process::ExitCode;
40	
41	use log::error;
42	
43	use crate::context::Context;
44	
45	/// Trait implemented by every subcommand's clap `Args` type so
46	/// `main.rs` can dispatch via a single `args.dispatch(&ctx)`
47	/// call.
48	pub trait SubcommandRunner {
49	    /// The clap-free `Params` struct the subcommand body
50	    /// consumes.
51	    type Params;
52	
53	    /// Build `Params` from `Args`. `Result<_, String>` absorbs
54	    /// both `From` (return `Ok(...)`) and `TryFrom` (forward
55	    /// the error) shapes uniformly.
56	    fn to_params(&self) -> Result<Self::Params, String>;
57	
58	    /// Run the subcommand body.
59	    fn run(ctx: &Context, params: &Self::Params) -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>>;
60	
61	    /// Whether to suppress the leading `vc-x1 X.Y.Z` banner.
62	    /// Default `false`; commands whose `Params` carry a
63	    /// banner-suppression flag (e.g. `chid` / `desc` / `list` /
64	    /// `show` under `-L`) override to read it.
65	    fn suppress_banner(_params: &Self::Params) -> bool {
66	        false
67	    }
68	
69	    /// Whether this invocation is the detached `finalize --exec`
70	    /// child. Default `false`; `finalize` (when ported) overrides
71	    /// to read it from its `Params`.
72	    fn is_detached_exec(_params: &Self::Params) -> bool {
73	        false
74	    }
75	
76	    /// Default dispatch: build `Params` via `to_params`, emit
77	    /// session chrome via [`crate::sb_ide`], run via `run`, and
78	    /// map the result to `ExitCode`. Errors at any stage log via
79	    /// `error!` and return `ExitCode::FAILURE`.
80	    fn dispatch(&self, ctx: &Context) -> ExitCode {
81	        let params = match self.to_params() {
82	            Ok(p) => p,
83	            Err(e) => {
84	                error!("{e}");
85	                return ExitCode::FAILURE;
86	            }
87	        };
88	        crate::sb_ide(
89	            Self::suppress_banner(&params),
90	            Self::is_detached_exec(&params),
91	        );
92	        match Self::run(ctx, &params) {
93	            Ok(()) => ExitCode::SUCCESS,
94	            Err(e) => {
95	                error!("{e}");
96	                ExitCode::FAILURE
97	            }
98	        }
99	    }
100	}
101

## refactor: move bm_track into dispatch (0.50.0-7)

Cycle cleanup (formerly the `-K` placeholder).
With every subcommand on `SubcommandRunner::dispatch`,
the top-level `is_detached_exec` match and the
`bm_track` enter/exit gates that consumed it no longer
need to live in `main`. Move them inside `dispatch`,
where they read the trait's `is_detached_exec(&params)`
directly. `main.rs`'s `fn main` collapses to:

```
let cli = …;
let _log = …;
let ctx = Context::load(…);
match cli.command { Commands::X(args) => args.dispatch(&ctx), … }
```

— roughly the shape the cycle was aiming for. The
twelve-arm match itself stays (compile-time
exhaustiveness on `Commands`); each arm is a single
`args.dispatch(&ctx)` line, and `main` does no other
work.

`bm_track` becomes `pub` so `dispatch` can call it
across the module boundary. The function name lookup
(`std::env::args().nth(1)`) moves into `dispatch`
unchanged — clap has validated the positional by the
time `dispatch` runs.

- `Cargo.toml`: `0.50.0-6` → `0.50.0-7`.
- `src/main.rs`: `fn bm_track` → `pub fn bm_track`;
  top-level `let is_detached_exec = match …` block,
  `let command_name = …` line, and both `if
  !is_detached_exec { bm_track(…) }` blocks deleted;
  the `let exit_code = match … ; … ; exit_code`
  pattern collapses to a single `match` expression.
- `src/subcommand.rs`: `dispatch` now resolves
  `is_detached_exec` once, threads it into both
  `sb_ide` and the `bm_track` enter/exit gates;
  trait-method doc on `is_detached_exec` expanded to
  mention the second consumer; dispatch doc expanded
  to cover the new bracket.
- `notes/chores-10.md`: backfilled `Commits: [[21]]`
  on the 0.50.0-6 section.
- `notes/todo.md`: 0.50.0-6 → `(done)`; rename
  `0.50.0-K …` line to `0.50.0-7 main.rs dispatch
  rework (current)`.

# References

content.text:
Work complete. `main`'s body is now just `Context::load` plus a thin match dispatch. `bm_track` enter/exit gates live in `SubcommandRunner::dispatch` and read `is_detached_exec` off the trait.

Proposed commit title and body:

```
refactor: move bm_track into dispatch (0.50.0-7)
```

```
Cycle cleanup (formerly the `-K` placeholder). With
every subcommand on `SubcommandRunner::dispatch`, the
top-level `is_detached_exec` match and the `bm_track`
enter/exit gates that consumed it no longer need to
live in `main`. Move them inside `dispatch`, where
they read the trait's `is_detached_exec(&params)`
directly.

`main.rs`'s `fn main` collapses to a `Context::load`
plus a thin twelve-arm dispatch match — each arm a
single `args.dispatch(&ctx)` line, and `main` does no
other work. The match itself stays for compile-time
exhaustiveness on `Commands`. See
`notes/chores-10.md > refactor: move bm_track into
dispatch (0.50.0-7)` for the full record.

- Cargo.toml: 0.50.0-6 → 0.50.0-7.
- src/main.rs: top-level `is_detached_exec` match,
  `command_name` line, and `bm_track` enter/exit
  gates deleted; `fn bm_track` made `pub`.
- src/subcommand.rs: `dispatch` reads
  `is_detached_exec` once and threads it through
  `sb_ide` plus the new `bm_track` enter/exit
  brackets.
- notes/chores-10.md: backfilled `Commits: [[21]]`
  on the 0.50.0-6 section.
- notes/todo.md: 0.50.0-6 → (done); rename `K` line
  to `0.50.0-7`.
```

OK to push?

file.content:
# Todo

This file contains near term tasks with a short description
and reference links to more details.

## In Progress

A bulleted list of the in-progress task's development "ladder":
   - 0.xx.y-0 blah (done)
   - 0.xx.y-1 blah blah (current)
   - 0.xx.y-2 blah blah blah
   - 0.xx.y close-out and validation

**Subcommand trait sweep** — collapse the per-arm
`Context::load` + `try_from` + `run_command` boilerplate in
`main.rs` behind a `Subcommand` trait. Design in
[chores-10.md](chores-10.md#chore-open-subcommand-trait-sweep-0500-0).
Per-step evaluation gate: any substep may modify the shape
significantly or abandon the cycle.

- 0.50.0-0 plan + version bump + chores section + todo ladder
  + linkme/inventory todos (done)
- 0.50.0-1 add `subcommand.rs` (trait) + port `chid` (done)
- 0.50.0-1.1 dicom-rs gotchas → todo (done)
- 0.50.0-1.2 --exec doc + matches! → match (done)
- 0.50.0-2 hoist `Context::load` + `dispatch(&Context)` (done)
- 0.50.0-3 port `desc` (done)
- 0.50.0-4 port `list` (done)
- 0.50.0-5 port 8 subcommands (show + 7 From) (done)
- 0.50.0-6 port `finalize` (`is_detached_exec` plumbing) (done)
- 0.50.0-7 main.rs dispatch rework (current)
- 0.50.0 close-out

## Todo

A markdown list of tasks to do in the near future, ordered
highest-priority first. Keep entries brief — 1-3 lines.
Detailed motivation, safety requirements, and ordering belong
in `notes/chores-NN.md` design subsections; link via `[N]` ref.

Items use lazy numbering — every entry begins with `1. `; the
markdown renderer auto-numbers them, so reorder/insert without
renumbering. Reference by displayed number ("let's work on #3").
1. **single-field `options_flags` leaves → `value` field.**
   `0.47.0` introduced the convention (single-field leaf names
   its field `value`, declares the flag via `#[arg(long = "…")]`,
   so consumers read `args.<leaf>.value` not `args.<leaf>.<leaf>`)
   on the new `squash` leaf. Sweep the pre-existing single-field
   leaves to match: `scope`, `repo`, `dry_run`, `private`,
   `account`, `config`, `use_template` + their consumers

project-local path without exporting `TMPDIR` globally.
   Open question: do we also expose a CLI parameter
   (e.g. `vc-x1 --workspace-tmp …`)? Test binaries can't
   easily accept arbitrary flags via `cargo test --`, so
   env is the realistic surface for tests; for the
   `vc-x1` binary itself a flag is feasible but unclear
   it adds value over the resolution chain.

## Done

Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](done.md) to keep this file small.

- `notes/todo.md`: 0.50.0-6 → `(done)`; rename

  rework (current)`.

## chore: close Subcommand trait sweep (0.50.0)

Cycle close-out. All 12 subcommands now run through
`SubcommandRunner::dispatch`; `main.rs`'s `fn main`
is `Context::load` + a 12-arm dispatch match and
nothing else. The per-arm boilerplate the cycle set
out to remove (`Context::load` + `try_from` +
`run_command`, ~12 lines × 12 arms) is gone; each arm
is a single `args.dispatch(&ctx)` line. The
`Commands` enum stays the dispatch source of truth
(compile-time exhaustiveness preserved), so the
`linkme` / `inventory` follow-up items in `## Todo`
remain queued as higher-leverage alternatives if the
per-arm cost ever feels burdensome again.

### As-built ladder

- 0.50.0-0 plan + version bump + chores section +
  todo ladder + linkme/inventory todos
- 0.50.0-1 add `subcommand.rs` (`SubcommandRunner`
  trait) + port `chid` (worked example, three
  material design calls landed: trait rename
  `Subcommand` → `SubcommandRunner` to dodge
  `clap::Subcommand` collision; session-chrome
  extracted as `pub fn sb_ide`; peek methods read
  from `Params`)
- 0.50.0-1.1 dicom-rs gotchas → todo (inter-substep
  tidy)
- 0.50.0-1.2 --exec doc + matches! → match
  (inter-substep tidy)
- 0.50.0-2 hoist `Context::load` to a single site in
  `main`; change `dispatch` to take `&Context`
- 0.50.0-3 port `desc`
- 0.50.0-4 port `list`
- 0.50.0-5 bulk port: `show` + 7 `From` commands
  (`validate_desc`, `fix_desc`, `clone`, `init`,
  `symlink`, `sync`, `push`); top-level
  `suppress_banner` match deleted
- 0.50.0-6 port `finalize` (`is_detached_exec`
  override wires `params.exec` through the trait);
  `fn run_command` deleted from `main`
- 0.50.0-7 move `bm_track` enter/exit into
  `dispatch`; delete the top-level
  `is_detached_exec` match in `main` (was the `-K`
  placeholder)

### Outcome

The trait approach landed cleanly — none of the
per-substep evaluation gates fired the "modify shape
significantly" or "abandon" outcomes the open
section reserved. The shape settled at `-1` (with the
three design calls noted above) held for the
remaining 10 ports without revision. `linkme` /
`inventory` would have gone further (eliminate the
`Commands` enum entirely), but at compile-time
exhaustiveness and macro-magic costs; they remain
queued in `## Todo` as separate items.

- `Cargo.toml`: `0.50.0-7` → `0.50.0` (suffix
  dropped — cycle close marker).
- `notes/chores-10.md`: backfilled `Commits: [[22]]`
  on the 0.50.0-7 section; new close-out section
  with the as-built ladder + outcome notes.
- `notes/todo.md`: deleted the In Progress ladder
  block; added `Subcommand trait sweep — 12
  subcommands ported via SubcommandRunner trait,
  main.rs collapsed to Context::load + thin dispatch
  (0.50.0)` to `## Done` with `[[37]]` ref.

# References

First chores-10 is "full" (1000+ lines)  whatever we do next let's start chores-11.

I'd like to eliminate sb_ide and SubcommandRunner::is_detached and suppress_banner.

Let's start with is_detached asi it's used dispatch; it controls invocation of bm_track what if bm_track was debug only i.e. -v?

200	        Stages: preflight (fmt/clippy/test) → review (approve diff)\n\
201	        → message ($EDITOR / --title+--body, approve text) →\n\
202	        commit-app → commit-claude (skipped if clean) → bookmark-both\n\
203	        → push-app → finalize-claude. Failures in commit-app /\n\
204	        commit-claude / bookmark-both roll both repos back via\n\

213	}
214	
215	/// Session chrome: emit the leading banner (unless suppressed) and
216	/// surface any failures left over from the previous run, gated on
217	/// "this isn't the detached `finalize --exec` re-entry".
218	///
219	/// Called from the trait's default `dispatch` (for ported
220	/// subcommands, reading the bools from each command's `Params`)
221	/// and from each unported arm in `fn main()` (reading the bools
222	/// from the per-arm peek match). When all arms are ported, the
223	/// `main`-side callers disappear; only the trait callers remain.
224	pub fn sb_ide(suppress_banner: bool, is_detached_exec: bool) {
225	    if !is_detached_exec {
226	        if !suppress_banner {
227	            // Banner on every normal run, mirroring what `--help`
228	            // shows at the top.
229	            log::info!("{BANNER}");
230	        }
231	        finalize::surface_previous_failures();
232	    }
233	}
234	
235	/// Permanent sanity check for the `main`-bookmark tracking state
236	/// in both repos of the dual-repo workspace. Prints one line on
237	/// entry and one on exit of every command. If entry and exit
238	/// differ, the executing command is the culprit; if entry differs
239	/// from the previous command's exit, something *between*
240	/// invocations broke it. Originally added in 0.37.2 as a temporary
241	/// diagnostic; promoted to permanent in 0.37.4 after the user
242	/// reported "happens more than once" — the signal is worth keeping
243	/// in place until confidence in its correctness is well-established
244	/// across varied use, at which point a "silent when clean"

content.text:
Moving `bm_track` to `debug!` works — `-v` runs see it, default runs don't, and the detached child (no `-v`) wouldn't print anyway, so the `if !is_detached` gates around `bm_track` become dead and can go. That's a clean step.

But it doesn't yet kill `is_detached_exec` on the trait — `sb_ide` still consumes it for two things (suppress the `vc-x1 X.Y.Z` banner, and gate `finalize::surface_previous_failures`). Both have meaningful reasons to skip in the detached child (chatter in its log; failures meant for next interactive run shouldn't get consumed by the child).

So shape suggestion for 0.51.0 (multi-step, chores-11):

- **0.51.0-0** plan + version bump + chores-11.md + design subsection on the trio (`sb_ide` / `is_detached_exec` / `suppress_banner`)
- **0.51.0-1** `bm_track` → `debug!`, drop the gates around it in `dispatch`
- **0.51.0-2..N** eliminate `sb_ide` — likely banner-on-every-run goes away (clap's `--help` pre_help already shows it), `surface_previous_failures` moves to finalize-side
- **0.51.0-3..N** drop `is_detached_exec` from trait, then `suppress_banner` from trait
- **0.51.0** close-out

Want me to open the cycle at 0.51.0-0 with chores-11 + this plan?

input.content:
# Chores-11

Continuation of `chores-10.md` (which is closed at `0.50.0` —
the Subcommand trait sweep). This file covers the `0.51.0`
cycle onward. Reference numbering is file-local — see
[`README.md`](README.md#reference-numbering); chores-11
starts at `[1]`.

## chore: open sb_ide elimination (0.51.0-0)

Multi-step. The `0.50.0` cycle landed every subcommand on
`SubcommandRunner::dispatch`, with two trait peek methods
(`suppress_banner` / `is_detached_exec`) and one free function
(`sb_ide`) carrying the per-run "session chrome" — the
`vc-x1 X.Y.Z` banner, `finalize::surface_previous_failures`,
and the bm_track enter/exit pair. This cycle eliminates all
three by relocating each behavior to where it actually
belongs:

- The `vc-x1 X.Y.Z` banner already lands on `--help` via
  clap's `before_help`; printing it again at the top of every
  normal run is duplicate chatter the user can recover from
  `--version`. Drop the on-every-run banner.
- `finalize::surface_previous_failures` is a finalize-side
  concern; moving its trigger into `finalize` itself (rather
  than into a generic dispatch hook) cleans up the gate.
- `bm_track` enter/exit is a diagnostic; demoting to `debug!`
  matches the "permanent sanity check, but quiet by default"
  intent already recorded in its module doc.

After these relocations the trait's `suppress_banner` /
`is_detached_exec` methods have no remaining consumers, and
`sb_ide` has no body left to keep — all three disappear.
Plan-only opener; the work lands in the substeps below.

### What goes away — and the reasoning

- **`bm_track` info-level emission.** Currently emits
  `bm-track enter vc-x1 X: app(main)=tracked, …` on every
  run. The module doc already notes "a future refinement
  would remove the steady-state noise without losing
  detection value" — `debug!` accomplishes exactly that.
  Detection value is preserved (run with `-v` when
  investigating); steady-state output gets cleaner.
- **`sb_ide`'s banner-on-every-run.** `clap::Command::before_help`
  already shows the banner on `--help`. The on-every-run
  emission is duplicate; users who want it explicitly have
  `vc-x1 -V`. Removing it shrinks the default output by one
  line per command.
- **`finalize::surface_previous_failures` gated in `sb_ide`.**
  This is finalize machinery — it reads `.vc-x1/finalize-fail-*`
  markers and surfaces them. The "don't surface from the
  detached child" gate is finalize's concern, not the trait's;
  moving the trigger into `finalize` itself lets the gate live
  where the marker logic lives.
- **`SubcommandRunner::is_detached_exec`.** With `bm_track`
  at `debug!` and `surface_previous_failures` relocated, no
  remaining consumer needs the trait's detached-exec peek.
  Drop the method; `finalize::is_detached_exec` (or
  equivalent) is a finalize-private concern.
- **`SubcommandRunner::suppress_banner`.** With the banner
  gone from `sb_ide`, the `-L` flag still suppresses bookmark
  labels in the listing subcommands' output (chid/desc/list/
  show via `CommonParams::header`) — that already lives in
  the op layer, not the chrome layer. The trait peek has no
  remaining consumer; drop it.

outcomes at any step boundary:

# Chores-11

Continuation of `chores-10.md` (which is closed at `0.50.0` —
the Subcommand trait sweep). This file covers the `0.51.0`
cycle onward. Reference numbering is file-local — see
[`README.md`](README.md#reference-numbering); chores-11
starts at `[1]`.

## chore: open sb_ide elimination (0.51.0-0)

Multi-step. The `0.50.0` cycle landed every subcommand on
`SubcommandRunner::dispatch`, with two trait peek methods
(`suppress_banner` / `is_detached_exec`) and one free function
(`sb_ide`) carrying the per-run "session chrome" — the
`vc-x1 X.Y.Z` banner, `finalize::surface_previous_failures`,
and the bm_track enter/exit pair. This cycle eliminates all
three by relocating each behavior to where it actually
belongs:

- The `vc-x1 X.Y.Z` banner already lands on `--help` via
  clap's `before_help`; printing it again at the top of every
  normal run is duplicate chatter the user can recover from
  `--version`. Drop the on-every-run banner.
- `finalize::surface_previous_failures` is a finalize-side
  concern; moving its trigger into `finalize` itself (rather
  than into a generic dispatch hook) cleans up the gate.
- `bm_track` enter/exit is a diagnostic; demoting to `debug!`
  matches the "permanent sanity check, but quiet by default"
  intent already recorded in its module doc.

After these relocations the trait's `suppress_banner` /
`is_detached_exec` methods have no remaining consumers, and
`sb_ide` has no body left to keep — all three disappear.
Plan-only opener; the work lands in the substeps below.

### What goes away — and the reasoning

- **`bm_track` info-level emission.** Currently emits
  `bm-track enter vc-x1 X: app(main)=tracked, …` on every
  run. The module doc already notes "a future refinement
  would remove the steady-state noise without losing
  detection value" — `debug!` accomplishes exactly that.
  Detection value is preserved (run with `-v` when
  investigating); steady-state output gets cleaner.
- **`sb_ide`'s banner-on-every-run.** `clap::Command::before_help`
  already shows the banner on `--help`. The on-every-run
  emission is duplicate; users who want it explicitly have
  `vc-x1 -V`. Removing it shrinks the default output by one
  line per command.
- **`finalize::surface_previous_failures` gated in `sb_ide`.**
  This is finalize machinery — it reads `.vc-x1/finalize-fail-*`
  markers and surfaces them. The "don't surface from the
  detached child" gate is finalize's concern, not the trait's;
  moving the trigger into `finalize` itself lets the gate live
  where the marker logic lives.
- **`SubcommandRunner::is_detached_exec`.** With `bm_track`
  at `debug!` and `surface_previous_failures` relocated, no
  remaining consumer needs the trait's detached-exec peek.
  Drop the method; `finalize::is_detached_exec` (or
  equivalent) is a finalize-private concern.
- **`SubcommandRunner::suppress_banner`.** With the banner
  gone from `sb_ide`, the `-L` flag still suppresses bookmark
  labels in the listing subcommands' output (chid/desc/list/
  show via `CommonParams::header`) — that already lives in
  the op layer, not the chrome layer. The trait peek has no
  remaining consumer; drop it.

### Ladder

- 0.51.0-0 plan + version bump + this section + todo
  ladder (current)
- 0.51.0-1 `bm_track` → `debug!`; drop the bm_track gates
  in `SubcommandRunner::dispatch`
- 0.51.0-2..N eliminate `sb_ide`:
  - Drop banner-on-every-run from `sb_ide`
  - Move `finalize::surface_previous_failures` trigger
    into `finalize` itself
  - Delete `sb_ide` once both bodies have moved
- 0.51.0-K drop `SubcommandRunner::is_detached_exec` (no
  remaining consumer) and `SubcommandRunner::suppress_banner`
  (no remaining consumer); drop `suppress_banner` fields
  from the four `Params` types that carry them
  (`ChidParams`, `DescParams`, `ListParams`, `ShowParams`).
- 0.51.0 close-out

### Per-step evaluation

Effectiveness is evaluated after each substep. Possible
outcomes at any step boundary:

- Continue as planned.
- Significantly modify the shape (e.g. keep the banner
  somewhere else; preserve `is_detached_exec` for a use
  case discovered mid-cycle) — recorded in a new chores
  subsection at the next step.
- Abandon: revert the cycle to a non-eliminated baseline,
  closing as a no-op with an `### Outcome` note capturing
  what didn't fit.

content.text:
Thoughts:

- The 11 Done entries span `0.44.0` → `0.50.0` — a coherent batch to archive together.
- Done-only refs in `todo.md` are `[1]`, `[2]`, `[3]`, `[30]`–`[37]`. The other refs (`[4]`–`[29]`) are still consumed by entries in `## In Progress` / `## Todo` and stay in `todo.md`.
- `done.md` ends at `[84]`; the migrated entries take `[85]`–`[95]`.
- `done.md` uses single-bracket `[N]` citations historically (markdown links — brackets don't render). Sticking with that style for the new entries avoids mixed-style churn in the archive file. The newer `[[N]]` convention is for live notes where you want brackets visible — appropriate for `todo.md` / `chores-*.md`, less so for an archive.
- Natural fit for this commit (`0.51.0-0`) — it's plan-only, no code changes, so todo.md / done.md hygiene rides along cleanly. The commit body / chores section gain one bullet noting the move.

OK to proceed with the move folded into 0.51.0-0?

content.text:
Done.md migration folded in. Updated commit body:

```
chore: open sb_ide elimination (0.51.0-0)
```

```
Multi-step cycle opener. The 0.50.0 cycle landed every
subcommand on `SubcommandRunner::dispatch` with two
trait peek methods (`suppress_banner` /
`is_detached_exec`) and one free function (`sb_ide`)
carrying per-run "session chrome" — the banner,
`finalize::surface_previous_failures`, and `bm_track`
enter/exit. This cycle eliminates all three by
relocating each behavior where it actually belongs:
banner already lives on `--help` (drop the on-every-run
emission), `surface_previous_failures` moves into
finalize, `bm_track` → `debug!`. After the relocations
the trait peeks have no remaining consumers and
`sb_ide` has no body left to keep.

Plan-only commit; the work lands across 0.51.0-N
substeps. See `notes/chores-11.md > chore: open
sb_ide elimination (0.51.0-0)` for the full design +
ladder. Done.md hygiene rides along — the 11 entries
spanning 0.44.0 → 0.50.0 migrate from todo.md ## Done
to done.md.

- Cargo.toml: 0.50.0 → 0.51.0-0.
- notes/chores-11.md: new file with the cycle opener.
- notes/chores-10.md: backfilled `Commits: [[23]]` on
  the 0.50.0 close-out section.
- notes/todo.md: new In Progress ladder for the
  cycle; ## Done entries (0.44.0–0.50.0) and their
  refs ([1], [2], [3], [30]–[37]) deleted with a
  one-line migration note.
- notes/done.md: 11 entries appended (renumbered
  [85]–[95]).
```

OK to push?
